event_blocks.summary: The FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019 match between the USA and Thailand commenced with the USA team, including Megan Rapinoe (jersey #15) and Alex Morgan (jersey #13), emerging from the tunnel alongside mascots and flag bearers to anthemic music and crowd cheers, with Rapinoe, as captain, shaking hands with officials. Following warm-ups and a brief shot of the USA coaching staff led by Jill Ellis, the game began with the USA attacking, leading to an early save by the Thai goalkeeper after a bobbled shot. The USA continued to press, and a goal was scored, credited to Alex Morgan, who celebrated with teammates amidst an electric stadium atmosphere. Later, Kelley O'Hara delivered a cross for a header, and Alex Morgan scored again, confirmed by the referee and scoreboard showing USA 1-0 THA at 13:33, prompting intense crowd reactions and replays of the goal past the diving Thai Goalkeeper. Commentator 2 praised O'Hara's aggressive play and Rapinoe's earlier drive, while Commentator 1 noted a turnover. The match continued with the USA maintaining pressure, as indicated by wide shots of the field and on-screen tournament graphics and sponsor advertisements.

Referee Team.description: Female referees in bright green uniforms, overseeing the match and signaling the goal after Alex Morgan's score.

USA Coaching Staff.description: Coach in a gray USA-branded jacket seated on the bench, displaying focused expressions and hand gestures during the pre-game moments.

Captain America Shield Icon.description: A graphic element appearing alongside the 'Viu Viu' text at the start, likely a thematic or branding addition.

Alex Morgan.description: USA player wearing a red jersey with the name 'MORGAN' and number 13, seen scoring a goal with a powerful strike and celebrating with a joyful leap and high-fives, with Commentator 1 confirming her name and the validity of her goal.

Thai National Team.description: Players in light blue jerseys, seen defending and reacting to the USA's attacks, including a player attempting to intercept the ball during a later play.

FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019.description: The event context, visible through on-screen graphics and banners, indicating the tournament and location, with time and score updates displayed.

Mascots and Flag Bearers.description: Young children in team-colored attire carrying national flags and accompanying the teams onto the field.

Stadium Crowd.description: Spectators in the stands waving American and Thai flags, creating a vibrant and energetic atmosphere, with one woman in a red USA jersey screaming in celebration, and their intense cheering and stadium roar clearly audible after the goal.

VAR Assistant Referee.description: Female assistant referee in a bright green uniform, holding a flag and signaling during the goal review process.

Thai Goalkeeper.description: Thai player in a yellow jersey, seen diving to save the goal but failing as the ball enters the net.

USA Player #3.description: USA player with blonde hair wearing a red jersey with the number 3, seen celebrating the goal by hugging Alex Morgan and participating in the team's jubilation.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a bright yellow screen displaying 'Viu Viu' in red, accompanied by a Captain America shield icon, set to upbeat electronic music. This transitions into a live broadcast of the FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019, as indicated by on-screen graphics and the FOX logo. A male commentator announces the 'teams making their way out onto the field as the US finally gets set to open up play here at the 2019 FIFA Women's World Cup,' while a wide aerial view shows the stadium. The USA team in red and the Thai team in light blue emerge from a tunnel, flanked by young mascots and officials carrying large national flags, to the sound of crowd cheering and an anthemic musical build-up. Close-ups reveal USA players like Megan Rapinoe, with her distinctive pink hair and jersey #15, and Alex Morgan in jersey #13. Rapinoe, as captain, shakes hands with the referee and the Thai captain, symbolizing sportsmanship, while the commentator notes Rapinoe is 'playing in her third World Cup.' The USA team warms up, with Rapinoe stretching and jogging. The camera briefly cuts to the focused USA coaching staff on the bench, including a coach in a gray USA-crested jacket, identified by the commentator as 'Jill Ellis, head coach.' The commentator also mentions 'Thailand' is playing in their 'second straight World Cup,' as the electric stadium atmosphere, filled with anticipation and crowd sounds, continues.

The segment opens with close-ups of focused USA players in red jerseys, including Alex Morgan (number 13) and a blonde player (number 3), as an announcer references past "World Cups." The announcer then introduces a Thai player, "Noon Gatai Satangian," returning for her second time. The scene transitions to a wide aerial view of the FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019 match between the USA in red and Thailand in light blue, with an excited announcer exclaiming, "Here we go!" The match begins with the USA attacking, and a shot on goal is taken, described by an announcer as "shooting it up," which the Thai goalkeeper saves after "bobbling" it. The action continues with the USA pressing forward, and a shot from "Mewis" (USA Player #3) is mentioned. The crowd's cheers intensify as a goal is scored, with an announcer excitedly detailing the play: "goalkeeper's coming out, look confused, headed, knocked in! US leads!" Alex Morgan celebrates with a joyful leap and high-fives teammates, and the announcer confirms, "Looks like Alex Morgan will get credit for that." The referee signals the goal, and the scoreboard updates to reflect the score. The electric atmosphere is palpable with the stadium crowd visible, waving flags and reacting to the play, alongside branded banners from sponsors like VISA and Qatar Airways.

The segment opens with the USA advancing the ball into Thailand's half, as Commentator 1 describes a "big cross" intended "for Morgan," which then falls "to Rapinoe," with "Lavelle" leading the attack before it's "cut out." The USA maintains pressure, and Commentator 1 excitedly narrates "O'Hara chipping it, back post header, GOAL!" The camera captures Alex Morgan scoring with a powerful strike, then leaping in joyful celebration with her arms raised, as Commentator 1 confirms, "Alex Morgan, this one will count." The scoreboard updates to USA 1–0 THA at 13:33, triggering an eruption of intense crowd cheering and a stadium roar. A woman in a red USA jersey is seen screaming in excitement amidst the jubilant Stadium Crowd, who wave flags. Replays show the ball entering the net past the diving but unsuccessful Thai Goalkeeper, and a slow-motion view of Morgan's strike. USA players, including USA Player #3, rush to embrace and high-five Morgan in celebration, while the Thai National Team players react with disappointment and the Referee Team signals the goal. Commentator 2 provides analysis, praising "such a good ball by Kelley O'Hara" and her "aggressive touch" that allowed her to "blow by the first defender," also noting Megan Rapinoe's earlier attempt to "drive in line." The scene shifts to a later moment where Kelley O'Hara, a USA player in red, slides to intercept the ball from a Thai player in light blue, a "challenge" highlighted by Commentator 2. The match continues with the USA maintaining pressure, visible in a wide shot of the field, as on-screen graphics display the FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019 tournament name, score, time, and advertisements for VISA, Qatar Airways, and KIA along the sidelines. Commentator 1 briefly notes a turnover, "giving it away in a bad spot."

event_blocks.summary: At 17:39 in the FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019 match, with the USA leading the Thai National Team 1-0, Alex Morgan, wearing red jersey #13, powerfully struck the ball past the diving Thai Goalkeeper for a goal, which the commentator initially attributed to Rose Lavelle, stating, "Shot taken! And Rose Lavelle made the right decision. Two nothing US!"; the score updated to USA 2-0 THA at 20:27. The stadium crowd erupted in sustained cheering and ambient sounds, including a woman in a red USA jersey screaming joyfully, as Alex Morgan celebrated by high-fiving teammates, including USA Player #3. The Referee Team, in bright green, signaled the goal, and the VAR Assistant Referee raised her flag for a check, with multiple replays confirming the ball crossed the line. The commentator further praised the goal, noting, "And it's a golden left foot, a Rose Lavelle," and later mentioned Kelly O'Hara's speed and Tobin Heath. The Thai National Team reacted with disappointment while USA players celebrated amidst banners for 'Qatar Airways' and 'Coca-Cola'. Later, the commentator noted a non-call by the referee after Sam Mewis went down, also mentioning Megan Rapinoe.

Referee Team.description: Female referees in bright green uniforms, overseeing the match and signaling the goal after Alex Morgan's score, with the VAR Assistant Referee raising her flag. Later, a commentator notes a 'referee is not calling that' in a subsequent play involving Sam Mewis.

USA Coaching Staff.description: Coach in a gray USA-branded jacket seated on the bench, displaying focused expressions and hand gestures during the pre-game moments.

Alex Morgan.description: USA player wearing a red jersey with the name 'MORGAN' and number 13, visually seen scoring a powerful goal with a strike past the Thai Goalkeeper and celebrating with a joyful leap and high-fives with teammates, despite the audio commentary attributing the goal to Rose Lavelle.

VAR Assistant Referee.description: Female assistant referee in a bright green uniform, holding a flag and signaling during the goal review process.

Thai National Team.description: Players in light blue jerseys, seen defending and reacting to the USA's attacks, including a player attempting to intercept the ball during a later play.

FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019.description: The event context, visible through on-screen graphics and banners, indicating the tournament and location, with time and score updates displayed.

Megan Rapinoe.description: USA player with distinctive pink hair, wearing a red jersey with the name 'RAPINOE' and number 15, seen warming up on the field and later celebrating the goal with teammates, and mentioned by commentators as being 'open' during a play and later receiving a pass.

Thai Goalkeeper.description: Thai player in a yellow jersey, seen diving to save the goal but failing as the ball enters the net.

USA Player #3.description: USA player with blonde hair wearing a red jersey with the number 3, seen celebrating the goal by hugging Alex Morgan and participating in the team's jubilation.

Stadium Crowd.description: Spectators in the stands waving American and Thai flags, creating a vibrant and energetic atmosphere, with one woman in a red USA jersey screaming in celebration, contributing to the sustained crowd cheering and stadium sounds heard throughout the goal celebration.

raw_segments: The video segment opens with a close-up of the USA Coaching Staff, a woman in a black jacket, seated in the stands and intently observing the match. On the field, the USA team in red jerseys is in possession, advancing against the Thai National Team in light blue, with the scoreboard showing USA 1-0 THA at 17:39. Commentary notes a previous shot was wide and mentions Sam Mewis. The action intensifies as Alex Morgan, wearing red jersey #13, visually scores a powerful goal with a strike, sending the ball past the diving Thai Goalkeeper. Simultaneously, the commentator exclaims, "Shot taken! And Rose Lavelle made the right decision. Two nothing US!", attributing the goal to Rose Lavelle. The score updates to USA 2-0 THA at 20:27. The stadium erupts with sustained crowd cheering and stadium sounds, including a woman in a red USA jersey screaming joyfully. Alex Morgan leaps in celebration, high-fiving teammates, including USA Player #3, who embraces her warmly. The Referee Team, in bright green, signals the goal, and the VAR Assistant Referee raises her flag, indicating a possible penalty check. Multiple replays of the goal from different angles confirm the ball crossing the line. The commentator further praises, "And it's a golden left foot, a Rose Lavelle," also mentioning Kelly O'Hara for her speed and Tobin Heath in subsequent plays. The Thai National Team players react with disappointment, while the USA players continue their jubilant celebration. The atmosphere is electric, with banners for 'Qatar Airways' and 'Coca-Cola' visible along the sidelines, and on-screen graphics consistently display 'FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019' as the time ticks from 17:39 to 23:28. Later, the commentator mentions Megan Rapinoe and Sam Mewis again, noting a non-call by the referee after Mewis went down.

event_blocks.summary: During the FIFA Women's World Cup, a Thai player in light blue contested a ball near the sideline against USA Player #3 in red, as commentators noted a potential VAR check amidst audible booing from USA fans. A VAR review was initiated by the referee in green for a USA player (number 9) falling near the goal after a challenge, while in a separate play, a curled ball was directed at 'Alisson Air.' The USA advanced, earning a free-kick outside the penalty area. From about 'a half yards away,' a low free-kick was sent in, becoming loose, before Horan scored, making it 3-0 for the USA, met with loud cheers and excitement from the stadium crowd. Megan Rapinoe and Alex Morgan, among other USA players, celebrated jubilantly, with USA Player #3 embracing Alex Morgan. The stadium crowd, waving American and Thai flags, reacted with palpable excitement, and a woman in a red USA jersey screamed amidst the roaring crowd. Replays showed the goal from multiple angles, illustrating the ball crossing the line despite the Thai goalkeeper's unsuccessful dive.

Referee Team.description: Female referees in bright green uniforms, overseeing the match and signaling the goal after Alex Morgan's score, including the VAR assistant referee holding a flag during the review.

Alex Morgan.description: USA player wearing a red jersey with the name 'MORGAN' and number 13, seen celebrating the goal with a joyful leap and high-fives, embracing USA Player #3, after Horan's goal.

VAR Assistant Referee.description: Female assistant referee in a bright green uniform, holding a flag and signaling during the goal review process.

Thai National Team.description: Players in light blue jerseys, seen defending and reacting to the USA's attacks, including a player attempting to intercept the ball during a later play.

FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019.description: The event context, visible through on-screen graphics and banners, indicating the tournament and location, with time and score updates displayed.

Megan Rapinoe.description: USA player with distinctive pink hair, wearing a red jersey with the name 'RAPINOE' and number 15, seen celebrating the goal with teammates, including hugging Alex Morgan and jumping in joy, amidst the loud stadium cheers.

Thai Goalkeeper.description: Thai player in a yellow jersey, seen diving unsuccessfully to save the goal, which is then confirmed by replays.

USA Player #3.description: USA player with blonde hair wearing a red jersey with the number 3, seen celebrating the goal by hugging Alex Morgan and participating in the team's jubilation, amidst the crowd's excitement.

Stadium Crowd.description: Spectators in the stands waving American and Thai flags, creating a vibrant and energetic atmosphere, initially heard 'booing' during the VAR discussion, and later reacting with loud cheers and excitement after the USA goal.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a dynamic sequence from the FIFA Women's World Cup, as a Thai player in a light blue jersey attempts to intercept the ball near the sideline, with a USA player (number 3) in red close by. Commentary discusses a potential VAR 'check or not,' while the 'USA fans booing' are distinctly audible. The scene quickly shifts to a split-screen VAR review, showing the FIFA VAR Room and on-field footage of a USA player (number 9) falling near the goal after a challenge, prompting the referee in bright green to signal for a review. Commentary then describes a separate play where the ball is 'cut wide' and 'curls it, but it's right at Alisson Air.' The action resumes with the USA team advancing, and a foul is called 'outside the penalty area,' leading to a free-kick. From approximately 'a half yards away,' the ball is sent in 'low,' becoming 'loose' before the commentator exclaims, 'And there's one for the US! It's three nothing Horan!' The scoreboard updates to 3-0 as the stadium erupts in loud cheers and excitement. USA players, including Megan Rapinoe (number 15) with distinctive pink hair and Alex Morgan (number 13), are seen erupting in joyful celebration, hugging and jumping. Close-ups capture the jubilant players, with USA Player #3 (blonde hair) embracing Alex Morgan. The stadium crowd, waving American and Thai flags, reacts with palpable excitement, and a woman in a red USA jersey is seen screaming in celebration amidst the roaring crowd. On-screen graphics display the match time, score, and tournament branding, including Coca-Cola, Qatar Airways, and Orange advertisements along the pitch. The sequence concludes with replays of the goal from multiple angles, highlighting the moment the ball crosses the line despite the Thai goalkeeper in yellow diving unsuccessfully.

event_blocks.summary: During the FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019 match against Thailand, the USA team achieved a record-breaking performance, culminating in a 13-0 victory. The first half saw the USA establish a commanding 3-0 lead with goals from Morgan (12'), Lavelle (20'), and Horan (32'). The second half witnessed an offensive onslaught, with Mewis scoring to make it 4-0, assisted by Rapinoe. Alex Morgan then netted her first of five goals, followed by another from USA Player #11, identified as Lavelle, bringing the score to 6-0, and then 7-0. The commentary highlighted Carli Lloyd's pursuit of a record and Alex Morgan's continued scoring, reaching a personal tally of five goals, tying Michelle Akers. Megan Rapinoe also contributed significantly, scoring multiple goals, including one that made it 9-0 and another that pushed the score to 10-0. USA Player #11, Pugh, scored her first World Cup goal, making it 11-0. The final goals came from Alex Morgan, her fifth, and Carli Lloyd, sealing the 13-0 final score. Throughout the match, the stadium crowd, including fans at the Lehigh Valley Soccerfest, provided a constant roar of support, while the Thai National Team, despite efforts from players like M. Chinwong, struggled to defend against the relentless USA attack, with the Referee Team and VAR Assistant Referee overseeing the play.

Referee Team.description: Female referees in bright green uniforms, overseeing the match and signaling the foul after a USA player is tackled near the penalty area.

USA Coaching Staff.description: Coach in a dark suit, seated on the bench, displaying a concerned expression during the match, later seen jumping in celebration with a blue jacket.

Lehigh Valley Soccerfest Crowd.description: A group of enthusiastic fans in Bethlehem, PA, celebrating the USA's 3-0 lead, waving flags, and cheering loudly, including one woman in a red USA jersey screaming in excitement, contributing to the electric atmosphere with their vocal support.

Thai National Team.description: Players in light blue jerseys, seen defending and reacting to USA's attacks, including a player attempting to intercept the ball during a later play and another being involved in a tackle that leads to a foul.

FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019.description: The event context, visible through on-screen graphics and banners, indicating the tournament and location, with time and score updates displayed, including the half-time score and goal scorers.

Thai Goalkeeper.description: Thai player in a yellow jersey, seen diving to save a shot but failing as the ball enters the net, and later standing in goal during the final moments of the half.

USA Player #3.description: USA player with blonde hair wearing a red jersey with the number 3, seen walking off the field during the half-time break, appearing composed after the team's dominant performance.

Stadium Crowd.description: Spectators in the stands waving American and Thai flags, creating a vibrant and energetic atmosphere, with their loud cheers and roars consistently heard, erupting in celebration after each USA goal.

Alex Morgan.description: USA player wearing a red jersey with the name 'MORGAN' and number 13, seen embracing Megan Rapinoe, scoring her fifth goal of the match with a powerful strike, and celebrating joyfully, with commentators exclaiming 'Five for Morgan!' and noting she's 'tying the great Michelle Akers with a five-goal performance'.

Megan Rapinoe.description: USA player with distinctive pink hair, wearing a red jersey with the name 'RAPINOE' and number 15, seen scoring a goal, celebrating with a joyful leap and high-fives, and later running in celebration after scoring.

USA Player #11.description: USA player in a red jersey with the number 11, seen scoring a goal with a well-placed shot, celebrating with teammates, and displaying joy and confidence on the field.

VAR Assistant Referee.description: Female assistant referee in a bright green uniform, holding a flag and signaling during the goal review process.

M. Chinwong.description: Thai player in a light blue jersey with the name 'M. CHINWONG' and number 3, seen looking dejected after a goal is scored against her team.

USA Player #8.description: USA player with distinctive blue hair, wearing a red jersey with the number 8, seen running in celebration after a goal.

raw_segments: The video segment captures the final moments of the first half of the FIFA Women's World Cup 2019 match between the USA and Thailand, with the scoreline at 3-0 in favor of the USA, set against the constant buzz and cheers of the stadium crowd. The action begins with the USA in possession, advancing the ball into Thailand's half, as a commentator describes "Done" keeping it low and "Morgan turning," attempting a shot that is blocked by the Thai defense. The camera follows the play closely, showing a Thai defender in light blue intercepting the ball and clearing it, while the commentator notes "nine shots for the US, five on goal." The USA continues to press, with multiple players in red jerseys making runs toward the goal, and the commentator highlights "Lavelle, rolling it" into the box. A key moment occurs when a USA player, identified by a commentator as "Horan" and later by another as "Lindsey" who "jumps into it and initiates the contact," is tackled near the penalty area, leading to a loud whistle and a foul call by the Referee Team in bright green uniforms. USA players react with frustration, while a commentator discusses the "push from behind" and another concludes there's "no penalty." The play continues with "Done" executing a "spin move" and taking a "shot," followed by "Rapino" sending the ball up, which is "headed by Lavelle" and then "Saved by the goalkeeper!" on a "rebound done." The scene transitions to the half-time break, with the scoreboard confirming the 3-0 lead and listing the goal scorers: Morgan (12'), Lavelle (20'), and Horan (32'). The camera then cuts to a crowd of enthusiastic fans at the Lehigh Valley Soccerfest in Bethlehem, PA, celebrating the USA's performance, waving flags, and cheering loudly, including one woman in a red USA jersey screaming in excitement, creating an electric atmosphere. The segment ends with a wide shot of the stadium, showing the players walking off the field as the half-time graphics display the score and goal details, with the crowd still audible.

The segment opens with a wide aerial view of the FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019 Group F match, showing the USA in red jerseys dominating Thailand in light blue, with the scoreboard indicating a 3-0 lead. The camera then cuts to the USA bench, where a coach and another woman clap, reflecting the team's strong momentum. A male commentator's voice guides the action, describing a play that leads to a pivotal moment: "Finds Mewis, Mewis with the shot deflected, GOAL!" The stadium erupts in a loud roar as the commentator exclaims, "Four nothing! The US!" Visually, Megan Rapinoe, identifiable by her pink hair and number 15 jersey, is seen celebrating joyfully with teammate Alex Morgan (#13), while the scoreboard updates to 4-0. A replay of the goal shows Rapinoe's involvement in the powerful shot that beats the Thai goalkeeper in yellow, with the commentator clarifying that Sam Mewis, who he praises for her recent rise to stardom, scored the goal, assisted by Rapinoe. The electric atmosphere continues with fans waving American flags and cheering loudly. Soon after, another goal is scored, bringing the score to 5-0. The commentator describes the build-up, mentioning "Heath" and "Horan" before a "quick shot, GOAL!" and then confirms, "Alex Morgan's got two," aligning with the visual of Morgan scoring with a well-placed shot and celebrating with a joyful leap and high-fives. The USA maintains control of the field, with the scoreboard visible at 53:06. The segment concludes with USA Player #3, a blonde player, walking off the field during the half-time break, as the vibrant stadium, adorned with Qatar Airways and Wanda banners, buzzes with the continuous cheers of fans and the FOX broadcast logo indicating live coverage.

The FIFA Women's World Cup 2019 match against Thailand is in full swing, with the USA women's soccer team showcasing a dominant performance, as the score progresses from 5-0 to 7-0 amidst the loud cheering and roaring stadium ambiance. The action begins with USA player Alex Morgan (#13) powerfully striking the ball into the net, with the Thai goalkeeper in yellow diving but failing to stop it. The crowd erupts in celebration, with fans waving American flags and cheering loudly as Alex Morgan embraces a teammate and runs with arms raised in triumph. The commentator notes the "US lead" as another goal is scored by USA player #11, identified by the commentator as Lavelle, who scores after a well-placed shot, again past the Thai goalkeeper. The scoreboard quickly updates to 6-0, then 7-0, with the commentator exclaiming "Six nothing" and later "Seven nothing US" and confirming "Lavelle has two" goals, as the USA team maintains relentless pressure. Thai players in light blue are seen defending and reacting to the attacks, with one player attempting to intercept the ball and another involved in a tackle that leads to a foul, signaled by the female referee in green. The stadium is filled with energy, with banners for sponsors like Qatar Airways, Adidas, and EDF visible around the pitch, and the continuous roar of the crowd underscores the excitement as the Thai goalkeeper stands in goal and the match progresses into the 56th minute, with USA players displaying joy and confidence.

The segment opens with a close-up of the Stadium Crowd, where fans are visibly engaged, some with American flag face paint and others holding drinks, reflecting the intense atmosphere of the FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019 match. The scoreboard shows USA leading 7-0 against Thailand at 60:00. Commentary notes a "Lloyd's turn" and a "great goal" from earlier, adding that "she has 30 minutes to equal that." The camera then cuts to the field, where USA Player #11 is seen in action, followed by a wide shot of the pitch as the USA team advances. The Thai National Team, in light blue jerseys, attempts to defend, with commentary describing a shot "shooting low" that's "blocked" and "cleared out," with the "last touch by Chinwong." A subsequent shot is "well wide of the target." The commentary then shifts to a more conversational tone, remarking, "Well, you could laugh when you have nothing, right?" in reference to the lopsided score. Visually, USA Player #13, Alex Morgan, scores a goal with a powerful strike past the Thai Goalkeeper, celebrating with a joyful leap and high-fives. A close-up shows a dejected Thai player, M. Chinwong, while USA Player #8 with blue hair runs in celebration. The USA Coaching Staff appears concerned. Commentary then describes Carli Lloyd attempting to "turn and spin" and shooting, followed by a "save" that mentions "Morgan!" before going "wide." The match continues with Alex Morgan visually scoring again, the Thai Goalkeeper diving but failing to save, and the referee signaling the goal. Commentary highlights "so much offense out there tonight for the US," mentioning "Rapinoe" clearing the ball and later "Pugh taking out Ertz," leading to "an easy foul to call." The final moments show a tackle near the penalty area, resulting in a foul called by the Referee Team, with the VAR Assistant Referee holding a flag. The scoreboard updates to 7-0, and the crowd's energy remains high, with fans waving flags and cheering, as the commentary concludes with a mention of "Thailand."

The FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019 match between USA and Thailand showcases a dominant USA performance, with the score progressing from 7-0 to 9-0 amidst a vibrant stadium atmosphere filled with cheering spectators. The segment opens with a commentator discussing a potential booking and a quick play involving a player identified as 'Horan.' A powerful shot from USA's Alex Morgan (#13, in red) results in a goal, with the commentator excitedly exclaiming 'quick shot! And there's another! Alex Morgan!' and declaring 'It's her night.' The scoreboard updates to 8-0, and Morgan celebrates joyfully, raising her arms, while a dejected Thai defender, M. Chinwong (#3), walks away. The commentator highlights this as 'Most goals US has scored in a single World Cup game.' The action continues with the commentator mentioning a Thai player, possibly 'Intamee,' and praising 'O'Hara' for good defensive work. Maintaining relentless offensive pressure, USA's Megan Rapinoe (#15, with distinctive pink hair) scores another goal after a quick counter-attack, the ball rolling past the diving Thai goalkeeper. The commentator's excitement peaks, announcing 'Rapinoe, who runs on! Rapinoe with the goal! Nine nothing US!' Rapinoe celebrates with a leap and high-five as the Thai goalkeeper remains on the ground. On-screen graphics display the overwhelming score and tournament branding, including sponsor logos, as the USA players continue their advance, leaving a frustrated Thai defender (#7) in their wake, all underscored by the continuous crowd noise and enthusiastic commentary.

The segment opens with the loud, celebratory roar of the stadium crowd, their cheers accompanying the USA women's soccer team in red jerseys as they huddle in joy after a goal, while the Thai team in light blue appears dejected. The commentator's voice cuts through the excitement, noting the relentless pressure and exclaiming, "quick shot taken!" followed by "She's got four! Alex Morgan!" as USA's Alex Morgan (number 13) scores, celebrating with arms raised. The scoreboard updates to 10-0, intensifying the crowd's enthusiasm, with fans waving American flags. The commentator then describes a missed opportunity by "Carli Lloyd," remarking on its "uncharacteristic" nature. The action quickly shifts back to the USA's attack, with Morgan nodding the ball for "Pugh," USA Player #11, who takes a well-placed shot into an "open net." The commentator excitedly announces, "There's the 11! Her first World Cup goal!" The Thai players, including M. Chinwong (number 3), look disheartened amidst the USA's continued celebration. The electric atmosphere of the FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019 is palpable, with banners for sponsors and FOX broadcast graphics displaying the live time and the historic 11-0 scoreline.

The video segment captures the USA women's soccer team's dominant performance in the FIFA Women's World Cup 2019 match against Thailand, with the score escalating from 11-0 to 13-0 amidst a backdrop of loud crowd cheers and an excited commentator exclaiming, "up, debut!" The action begins with a USA player in red, Megan Rapinoe (#15), scoring a goal after a swift attack, followed by a close-up of her celebrating with teammates, including Alex Morgan (#13), who embraces her. The camera then cuts to the USA bench, where the coaching staff and substitutes, including a coach in a blue jacket, erupt in celebration. The scene shifts to a wide-angle view of the field, showing the USA team maintaining relentless pressure. The commentator's voice rises, announcing, "Morgan, shot! Goal!" as Alex Morgan scores, making it 12-0. The crowd's cheers are deafening as the commentator declares, "Five for Morgan!" and notes she's "tying the great Michelle Akers with a five-goal performance." A female commentator adds, "And this is just ridiculous," analyzing the play. The dominance continues, with the commentator building anticipation, "Lloyd has a chance here!" before exclaiming, "Carli Lloyd, she's got it!" as another USA player, identified as Carli Lloyd, scores past the diving Thai goalkeeper in yellow. The crowd erupts again, and the commentator announces the staggering score, "13 nothing US!" Rapinoe and Morgan are seen hugging and running joyfully amidst the sustained cheers. The on-screen graphics consistently display the match time, score, and tournament branding. The segment concludes with a dejected Thai player, M. Chinwong (#3), as the USA's confidence and skill remain on full display, cementing their overwhelming victory.

event_blocks.summary: The roar of the Stadium Crowd intensified as USA Player #10, identified as LLOYD, dribbled the ball and scored the final goal, the ball sailing past the diving Thai Goalkeeper. In stoppage time, with the scoreboard showing 92:01, commentators noted Lloyd's tactical shift into a 'double nine spot' and Pew's move to midfield. A distinct whistle signaled the 'Final whistle,' leading to an exclamation of 'What a night!' and the unprecedented '13 nothing result at a World Cup.' Players from the USA and Thai National Teams exchanged handshakes, showcasing sportsmanship. The final score, 'FULL TIME USA 13-0 THAILAND,' was displayed, listing goal scorers including Alex Morgan with 'five goals.' Megan Rapinoe, identifiable by her pink hair, and USA Player #8, with blue hair, embraced teammates and USA Coaching Staff, with one coach in a blue jacket jumping in celebration. The Thai Goalkeeper and a USA player walked off together in a gesture of mutual respect before the screen displayed a red 'Thanks For Watching Subscribe Now!' message.

Referee Team.description: Female referees in bright green uniforms, overseeing the final moments of the match and signaling the end of play, marked by a distinct 'Final whistle' sound.

USA Coaching Staff.description: Coach in a dark suit, later seen jumping in celebration with a blue jacket, embracing players after the match.

Alex Morgan.description: USA player wearing a red jersey with the name 'MORGAN' and number 13, seen celebrating with teammates and staff, including a joyful embrace with a coach in a blue jacket. A commentator explicitly credits her with scoring 'five goals' in the match.

Thai National Team.description: Players in light blue jerseys, seen shaking hands and showing sportsmanship with USA players after the match, including one player (M. Chinwong) looking dejected.

FIFA Women's World Cup France 2019.description: The event context, visible through on-screen graphics and banners, including the final score and goal scorers list, with time updates showing 92:01 to 93:05 and the 'FULL TIME' announcement. Commentators refer to it as 'a World Cup' and emphasize the rarity of '13 nothing results' in such a tournament.

USA Player #10.description: USA player wearing a red jersey with the name 'LLOYD' and number 10, seen dribbling the ball before a goal is scored and later shaking hands with the Thai goalkeeper. A commentator notes that 'Lloyd had gone back up into her double nine spot' during the match.

Megan Rapinoe.description: USA player with distinctive pink hair, wearing a red jersey with the name 'RAPINOE' and number 15, seen embracing teammates and staff in celebration after the match.

Thai Goalkeeper.description: Thai player in a yellow jersey, seen diving to save a shot but failing as the ball enters the net, and later standing in goal during the final moments, shaking hands with USA players.

USA Player #8.description: USA player with distinctive blue hair, wearing a red jersey with the number 8, seen running in celebration and embracing teammates after the match.

Stadium Crowd.description: Spectators in the stands waving American and Thai flags, cheering and celebrating the USA's victory, with one woman in a red USA jersey screaming in excitement. The audio features prominent crowd cheering and a commentator noting that 'USA fans are celebrating this one'.

raw_segments: The segment opens with the roar of a cheering crowd as USA Player #10, identified by a commentator as LLOYD, dribbles the ball and scores the final goal, the ball flying past the diving Thai Goalkeeper. Enthusiastic USA fans, including one in a patriotic hat, are seen cheering loudly in the stands. As the match enters stoppage time, the scoreboard displays 92:01, with commentators discussing Lloyd's tactical shift into a 'double nine spot' and another player, Pew, shifting into midfield. A distinct whistle signals the 'Final whistle,' prompting a commentator to exclaim, 'What a night!' and highlight the unprecedented '13 nothing results at a World Cup.' Players from both the USA and Thai National Teams, in their red and light blue kits respectively, begin to shake hands, displaying sportsmanship. The final score, 'FULL TIME USA 13-0 THAILAND,' is displayed alongside a scrolling list of goal scorers, including Alex Morgan, who a commentator explicitly credits with 'five goals.' USA players, including Megan Rapinoe with pink hair and USA Player #8 with blue hair, embrace teammates and staff, with a coach in a blue jacket jumping in celebration. The Thai Goalkeeper is seen walking off with a USA player, a gesture of mutual respect, before the screen transitions to a red 'Thanks For Watching Subscribe Now!' message.
